mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS transmits through lower discount rates and easier financial conditions, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next month. The signal is valid when policy is easing rather than merely stable, and it fails if rate cuts coincide with a recessionary spike in labor stress that overwhelms valuation support.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are negative or below the unconditional mean in easing episodes, or if the sign flips and remains negative across any 3-year rolling window with Rank IC below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, the market interprets policy as restrictive enough to compress equity multiples and slow risk-taking, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect should strengthen if CPIAUCSL is still elevated, because real rates and tightening expectations rise together.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the signal loses negative sign consistency when CPIAUCSL is above its recent trend.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above 4.0% marks deteriorating labor demand and weaker household income growth, which should depress cyclicals and broad index returns over the next quarter. The signal is strongest when PAYEMS is also flattening or falling, confirming that unemployment reflects real labor weakness rather than participation noise.
failure_condition: Reject if the next-60-day SPX return is positive in a majority of unemployment-break episodes, or if the predictive relation disappears when PAYEMS growth remains positive.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained improvement in RSAFS signals firmer nominal consumer spending and revenue growth, which supports earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 performance over the next month. The mechanism is falsified if sales growth fails to translate into positive returns after accounting for the level of inflation and policy rates.
failure_condition: Reject if periods of rising RSAFS are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in INDPRO signals factory and industrial slowdown that precedes weaker risk appetite and lower equity returns over the next month. The effect should be negative when industrial production contracts faster than household demand metrics, because supply weakness then dominates the market narrative.
failure_condition: Reject if two-month INDPRO declines do not correspond to below-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign turns positive in more than 40% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in HOUST below its recent trend indicates housing demand shock and tighter financial conditions, which should translate into weaker broad equity returns over the next quarter. The signal is most informative when it is accompanied by elevated FEDFUNDS, confirming that housing weakness is policy-sensitive rather than idiosyncratic.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% below its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if large housing contractions are not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal vanishes when FEDFUNDS is high.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS series indicates job losses and weaker payroll income, which should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next week as investors discount future earnings. The effect is short-horizon because labor data quickly alters recession probabilities and sector rotation.
failure_condition: Reject if short-run SPX returns are not negative after PAYEMS declines, or if the sign is positive in a rolling 2-year window despite repeated payroll contractions.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth is slowing while CPIAUCSL remains elevated, the market faces stagflation: weaker real activity with sticky inflation sustains rate pressure and lowers equity valuations. In that regime, S&P 500 returns over the next quarter should be negative, but the signal fails if inflation falls quickly enough to restore policy flexibility.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth turns negative while CPIAUCSL stays above its 12-month average
failure_condition: Reject if negative-growth/high-inflation episodes do not deliver below-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the interaction effect disappears when CPIAUCSL re-accelerates.
